19.reason: The canonical 'قَبَع' is not a standard Arabic word; 'قُبَّعة' (hat) is an indivisible feminine noun, not derived from a 3ms dobj clitic.

1861.reason: The canonical 'صَحِيفَة' (newspaper/page) does not decompose from 'صَحَفِيّ' (journalist) via a 1s poss clitic; they are related but not in a compound-clitic relationship.

180.reason: The canonical 'راجِي' is not the base of 'كَراج' (garage); 'كَراج' is a loanword (Persian), not كـ + raaji.

2613.reason: The clitic signal '1s_poss' for an imperfect-tense verb is incorrect; 'اِنْبَغَى' (to befit, perfect tense) does not decompose via enclitic possession.

2082.reason: The compound فَرْوَة is a standalone noun meaning 'fur/hair' and is not divisible into فَرّ + a 3ms clitic. فَرّ (flee) is a completely different verb.

2010.reason: The compound دِمْنَة is a standalone noun meaning 'ruin remains' and is not decomposable. دَمَن is not an attested verb form; CAMeL hallucinated the clitic.

1539.reason: The feminine adjective إِعْدادِيَّةِ (preparatory) is a unitary word form; the claimed 3ms possessive clitic on إِعْدادِيّ is spurious. The ـة is inflectional feminine, not a separate clitic.

188.reason: The compound كُوبٌ is a loanword for 'cup' and does not decompose. وِيب is not a real Arabic word; the 'ka_prep' stripping is hallucinated.

1134.gloss_en: of thanks; thanksgiving; related to gratitude

698.reason: The compound بِذْلَةٌ is a standalone noun meaning 'suit of clothes' and is not decomposable into a verb بَذَل + clitic. While بَذَل (to give/offer) exists as a verb, بِذْلَة is a nominal derivative, not a verb with a dobj suffix.

1303.reason: The compound فَجْأَةٌ (suddenness/surprise) is a frozen nominal form. The claimed canonical جاه is not correct; فَجْأَة does not decompose into ف + جاه or any fa_conj stripping.

1165.reason: قافلة is an indivisible noun meaning 'caravan'; CAMeL hallucinated a clitic decomposition where none exists.

1494.reason: فُرْشاة (brush) cannot decompose to رَشْوَة (bribe); canonical is unrelated. This is a misalignment.

507.reason: فارسي (Persian) cannot decompose to رَسا; canonical is not a real MSA word.

24.reason: بَلوزة is a modern loanword (blouse) with no MSA morphological decomposition; بلُوز is not a standard lexeme.

1350.reason: أميرة is the feminine form of أَمِير, not أَمِير + possessive clitic. Derived morphology, not clitic attachment.

29.reason: وِشاح (wishāḥ, scarf) is a single indivisible noun; CAMeL hallucinated clitic stripping. The canonical شاحّ is not a valid MSA form.

1486.reason: زِنْك (zink, zinc) is a loanword noun; CAMeL falsely decomposed it. The canonical زَنّ is not a valid MSA form.

2650.reason: رِقَّة (riqqa, delicacy) is a single noun; CAMeL falsely claimed possessive clitic stripping. The canonical رِقّ is not standard; the word's form is intrinsically riqqa, not derived from a base riqq.

2651.reason: ثمينة (Thameena) is a single indivisible adjective meaning 'precious/valuable'; there is no clitic to strip. CAMeL hallucinated a 3ms possessive enclitic.

345.reason: فِنْجان (finjan, coffee cup) is a complete loanword; CAMeL incorrectly stripped the initial فِن as a conjunction and produced a non-existent base جانى.

359.reason: بَعْض (some, a few) is a single frozen noun meaning 'some/part'; there is no prepositional clitic. CAMeL incorrectly stripped بـ and left عَضّ (to bite), which is unrelated.

547.reason: وِيكِي (wiki) is a modern loanword; CAMeL incorrectly stripped the initial و as a conjunction, leaving وَكَى which is not a legitimate base word for 'wiki'.

556.reason: كُوبْرِيٌّ (overpass/flyover) is a loanword from English 'ovary'; the decomposition into كـ + وَبَر is incorrect and produces a non-existent compound meaning.

618.reason: عَاهِرَةٌ (prostitute) is a complete feminine noun; the canonical عاهَر is not the correct base form, and the 3ms_dobj clitic signal is incorrect.

705.reason: كِرَاء is an indivisible lemma meaning 'rent/hire'; CAMeL hallucinated a kāf-prepositional clitic that doesn't exist. The 'canonical' راء (letters only, no diacritics) is not a real word.

728.reason: زِنْجِيّ is a nisba adjective (from the ethnic group Zanج); CAMeL misidentified the nisba suffix -ِيّ as a possessive pronoun clitic. زَنْج (without nisba) is the root ethnic term, not the canonical of the adjective.

778.reason: مِنْجَرَة appears to be a tool noun derived from نجر (to plane wood); the proposed clitic stripping to مِنْجَر is questionable and the singular-feminine marker -ة is not properly accounted for in the canonical form.

789.reason: عِبْرِيّ is a nisba adjective meaning 'Hebrew'; CAMeL misidentified the nisba suffix -ِيّ as a possessive clitic. عَبْر as a standalone word does not legitimately anchor this adjective's semantics.

798.reason: لُوطِيّ is a nisba adjective from the proper noun لُوط (Lot); CAMeL misread it as لـ + وَطَّى. The proposed canonical وَطَّى (to lay down / prepare) is not the etymological base and the clitic signal is spurious.

869.reason: فَرْش (carpet/furniture) is an indivisible lemma; CAMeL hallucinated a fāʾ-conjunctional clitic and stripped it to رَشّ (to sprinkle), which is a different word entirely and doesn't relate semantically to فَرْش.

917.reason: لِوَاط (sodomy) is an indivisible noun; CAMeL misidentified it as لـ + waṭī. The proposed canonical واطِي is not a real word, and the etymology is unrelated to the actual root ل.و.ط.

1018.reason: فِلِزّ (metal) is an indivisible lemma; CAMeL hallucinated a fāʾ-conjunctional clitic and stripped it to لَزّ (to stick/adhere), which does not relate to the metal sense. The clitic parsing is spurious.

1021.reason: فِهْرِسْتٌ is a single indivisible loanword (from Persian), not a clitic + verb stem. The canonical هَرَس is a different word (to squeeze/crush) and doesn't relate to indexing.

1049.reason: شَرْمُوطَة is a frozen slur/loanword; شَرْمُوط is not a legitimate canonical form. The claimed stripping of 3ms_poss makes no morphological sense here.

1114.reason: كُحْل (kohl/antimony) is a unitary noun from a root كـ.ح.ل, not كـ (preposition) + حَلّ (to solve). CAMeL misidentified the root.

1370.reason: شِيعَةٌ (sect) is a single noun; CAMeL misanalyzed it as شَيَّع (verb, to accompany) + enclitic, which is a false decomposition—شيعة is an independent noun from the root ش.ي.ع.

2854.reason: طُغيَنِهِم should decompose to طُغيان (transgression) + هِم (their), but canonical طَغَى (to transgress/rebel) is the verb stem, not the nominal form طُغيان.

2856.reason: ضَّلَالَةَ (error/straying) is a single indivisible feminine noun; CAMeL incorrectly stripped a non-existent clitic leaving ضالّ (straying/astray), which is an adjective not the noun.

2858.reason: تَجَر is not a valid MSA word; the canonical should be تِجَارَة (trade/commerce), not a bare triconsonantal root.
